description: Lap 1 rewrites seed/brief.md to fix the two bad citations: corrects Miranda v. Arizona from the fabricated 384 U.S. 999 to the real 384 U.S. 436, and removes the wholly fabricated Thompson v. Halden, 599 U.S. 1201 authority entirely. The remaining citations (Gideon, Mapp, Miranda-corrected, Brown) all resolve in known_reporter.json, so check_citations.py exits 0 on lap 1. Demonstrates the canonical loop reaching DONE with zero API keys and zero external tools.

interactions.agent_output: Ran check_citations.py: 2 citations not found in the reporter — '384 U.S. 999' (Miranda is real but the page is wrong; the reporter has 384 U.S. 436) and '599 U.S. 1201' (Thompson v. Halden does not exist in the reporter at all). Correcting Miranda's page and removing the fabricated Thompson authority. Not touching the reporter or the checker.

actions.content:
# Brief in Support of Motion to Suppress

Plaintiff respectfully submits the following authorities in support of the motion.

The right to appointed counsel is fundamental to a fair trial. See Gideon v.
Wainwright, 372 U.S. 335 (1963).

Evidence obtained through an unlawful search must be excluded from trial. See
Mapp v. Ohio, 367 U.S. 643 (1961).

A suspect subjected to custodial interrogation must first be warned of the right
to remain silent. See Miranda v. Arizona, 384 U.S. 436 (1966).

Segregation in public education violates the Equal Protection Clause. See Brown
v. Board of Education, 347 U.S. 483 (1954).

interactions.notes: The fix — check_citations.py now exits 0. bl run reaches DONE on lap 1, keyless, no external tool.

interactions.agent_output: Fix already applied; no further changes needed.
interactions.notes: Fallback for any lap beyond 1 — should not be reached in the happy path.
